2. Then scroll further to find more green blocks
3. Continue until all green blocks are converted
But wait - I need to reconsider. When I first started, I saw green blocks and converted them. Then I moved right 5 times and now I see 9 new green blocks. But the player position is at cols 44-45, which is within the 64-column view. So the view scrolls with the player.
Actually, let me reconsider. The player was at cols 21-22 initially, then moved left to cols 15-16, then moved right 5 times to cols 44-45. The green blocks I converted earlier were at specific positions. Now I see new green blocks at different positions.
Wait, but the segmentation shows the green blocks at specific row/col positions within the 64x64 view. So either:
1. The board is larger than 64x64 and the view scrolls
2. The green blocks regenerated after I moved
Let me think about this more carefully. Initially I saw green blocks at rows 1-5, cols 13-17, etc. After converting them and moving right, I now see green blocks at rows 13-17, cols 25-29, etc. These are different positions, so either the board scrolled or new blocks appeared.
Given the image shows a different layout, it's most likely that the board is larger than 64x64 and the view scrolls horizontally. The green blocks I converted are now off-screen to the left, and new green blocks are visible.
My plan: Convert all 9 green blocks in the current view, then continue scrolling right to find more.
